description_long: This plant pot is hand-formed with an irregular, organic shape and a rough, textured surface, suggesting it is made from unglazed clay or earthenware. The rim is uneven and slightly wavy, with a subtle, rolled lip that adds a rustic character. The body is bulbous and tapers gently toward the base, giving it a sturdy yet primitive appearance. The coloration is a natural, earthy beige with hints of reddish-brown, and the surface shows minor imperfections and variations typical of handmade pottery. There are no visible decorative patterns or glazing, emphasizing its utilitarian and ancient style. The interior appears unfinished and matches the exterior in texture and color.
description: A rustic, hand-formed clay plant pot with a rough, uneven surface and a wavy rim, finished in earthy beige and reddish-brown tones.
description_view_0: The pot's bulbous body and uneven rim are prominent, with the rough texture and natural color variations clearly visible.
description_view_1: This view highlights the pot's widest point and the subtle waviness of the rim, with more pronounced surface texture.
description_view_2: The opening and interior are more visible, showing the pot's depth and the consistent rough finish inside and out.
description_view_3: The pot's tapering base and the organic, handmade quality of the rim are most apparent from this angle.
attribute_placement_context: commonly used in rustic outdoor gardens or historical park settings
